questions.subject: History
questions.prompt: With reference to the UNESCO Creative Cities Network (UCCN), which of the following best describes its primary objective in the context of urban development?

options.text: To provide direct financial subsidies for the restoration of heritage buildings in metropolitan areas.
To integrate creativity and the creative economy into urban planning to achieve the UN 2030 Agenda.
To establish a global standard for the certification of traditional handicraft exports from developing nations.
To facilitate the migration of creative professionals from rural clusters to industrial urban centers.

questions.explanation: The UCCN was created in 2004 with the objective of placing creativity and the creative economy at the core of urban development plans. This is intended to make cities safe, resilient, inclusive, and sustainable, directly aligning with the UN 2030 Agenda for Sustainable Development.
questions.takeaway: The UCCN leverages the creative economy as a strategic tool for sustainable urban development in line with global SDG targets.
questions.evidenceExcerpt: Objective - Placing creativity and the creative economy at the core of their urban development plans to make cities safe, resilient, inclusive and sustainable, in line with the UN 2030 Agenda for Sustainable Development
